title: Vairagad Fort
tagline: Vairagad Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Vairagad is a small fort situated in the Gadchiroli district of Maharashtra, India, at the confluence of rivers Khobragadhi and Satnalas.
overview.description: Vairagad is a small fort situated in the Gadchiroli district of Maharashtra, India, at the confluence of rivers Khobragadhi and Satnalas. The small dusty village of same name is adjacent to the fort. The fort is situated 180 km from Nagpur and 80 km from Chandrapur.

topPlaces.name: Kurkheda
topPlaces.description: Kurkheda is a Village and a tehsil in the Gadchiroli district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. According to the 2011 census the village has a population of 379 living in 90 households.

items.note: Kurkheda is a Village and a tehsil in the Gadchiroli district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. According to the 2011 census the village has a population of 379 living in 90 households.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vairagad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vairagad Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vairagad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur (131 km) and the nearest railway station is Nagpur Junction (131 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vairagad Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vairagad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vairagad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Vairagad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
